note: Federal trade secret protection that can run alongside a contractual confidentiality clause.
Adopted, with variation, by most U.S. states as the baseline for trade secret definitions and remedies.
Harmonized EU baseline for trade secret protection.

citation: PepsiCo, Inc. v. Redmond, 54 F.3d 1262 (7th Cir. 1995)
note: Established the inevitable disclosure doctrine under Illinois Trade Secrets Act.
Delineates the limits of trade secret protection against an employee's general memory.
Disclosure compelled by legal process (e.g., subpoena, court order) is a recognized limit on confidentiality duties, commonly conditioned on prior notice to the disclosing party.

citation: Restatement (Third) of Unfair Competition § 42 cmt. c
note: Post-relationship return/destruction obligations for confidential materials are a standard feature of confidentiality doctrine to prevent continued unauthorized use after the relationship ends.
